post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 315 (Newbie Game 119):
Baking Mafia

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
  • Voting/Elimination Procedures
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang/elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no player has been hanged, the amount of votes required for a Hang or a No Hang will be reduced by 1. The vote threshold will reduce by 1 vote every 24 hours after this. For example, if there are 8 players alive in the game, 5 votes will be required to hang someone and 4 votes are required for a no hang. Once the deadline passes, 4 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 3 votes are sufficient for a no hang. 24 hours after the deadline, 3 votes will be sufficient to hang someone and 2 votes are sufficient for a no hang.
    • 2.10: If the vote threshold is decreased and two or more options have both reached the threshold required, then the game will enter tiebreak. During tiebreak, votes can continue to be changed. If at any point in time, only one option has enough votes to meet the threshold required for the option to be enacted, day will immediately end. Or, if any option gains votes and now exceeds the vote threshold by an amount of votes strictly greater than the amount of votes any other option exceeds their vote threshold by, that option will win and day will immediately end. If 24 hours have passed since tiebreak has begun, and the votes are still deadlocked, there will be no hang.
    • 2.11: After an option has won the vote, it will become twilight and players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 168 hours (1 week). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 168 hours has elapsed.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, they must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I, BananaBall00, am the moderator of Mafia Game 315
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are All Purpose Flour (Vanilla Townie).



You are a must-have in every baker's pantry. There is no baking without you. Like your name suggests, you can be used in almost every recipe and you are also the standard flour being used in baking recipes. One can bake cinnamon rolls (external link) with all-purpose flour.

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Water, (Town Cop).



Water is one of the main ingredients of bread. But not only that, you are also very helpful for testing to make sure other baking ingredients work. For example, you are used to proof yeast and can also be used to test freshness of chemical leaveners. Without you, baking would be a lot harder because one would be unsure if their hard work would result in something edible. Here is a tutorial (external link) on how to test chemical leaveners for freshness using water.

Each night you may visit a player and perform small tests on them, determining if they are fit for baking.

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Granulated Sugar (Town Doctor).



Does something taste bad? Add sugar. Is there not enough flavor? Add sugar. You fix everything. There is no recipe without you. There is no baking without your flavor enhancement, because no one will eat anything without your magical sugar powers. You stand out in sugar cookies (external link).

Every night you may choose a player to protect them from being spoiled by fake baking ingredients.

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Butter (Town Tracker).



The moment a baker lays even a finger on you, there will be oily fingerprints everywhere, on the kitchen counter and on the side of every bowl. You're very hard to get rid of - you can't go down the drain but you're also very hard to wipe or wash off. But the best part is that many recipes include you anyways. One can bake butter cookies (external link) with butter - your rich flavor stands out in those.

Every night you may target someone with your ultra smooth skills and find out where they have been (who they visited).

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Yeast (Town Jailkeeper).



There is no bread without you. You shine in almost every bread recipe, making dense lumps of dough into light, airy, delicious bread. You have a purpose, though. During the process of fermentation, carbon dioxide bubbles are trapped in the dough, causing it to rise, but you can also trap other ingredients inside the unyielding network of gluten in the dough. One can bake artisan bread* (external link) with yeast.

Each night you may choose someone to put in your dough jail. That person will be unable to perform any actions that night and nightkills attempting to target that person will be blocked.

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.

*this is literally my favorite recipe you should try it :>
Quote:
You are Cinnamon (Town 1 Shot Bulletproof).



Not only does your flavor stand out in a lot of recipes, you are also special in your very own way. You act as a natural preservative for dough - slowing down fermentation to prevent premature spoilage. One can use cinnamon to make apple cinnamon bread (external link).

You are able to prevent yourself from being spoiled by fake baking ingredients one time.

You win when all of the fake baking ingredients (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Tomato (Mafia Goon).



You don't get it. Why can't tomato be used for baking? You keep mentioning that tomatoes can be used for pizza, and pizza crust can be baked, but the other ingredients simply don't understand. You feel like an outsider and wish seek revenge against the baking ingredients who rejected your presence, no matter the cost.

Together with your partner (), you must eliminate all the typical baking ingredients from the kitchen. That way, when the baker comes back, they will have no choice but to use you as a baking ingredient. Each night, you may decide with your partner on which ingredient to eliminate, removing them from the kitchen. You may speak with your partner here.

You win when all of the typical baking ingredients (Town) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Cheese (Mafia Roleblocker).



Like your Tomato friend, you don't understand why you can't be used for baking. You are pretty sure lots of people enjoy savory pastries that involve cheese. But these typical baking ingredients simply are not willing to accept you and your friend, so the only solution is to force your way in.

Together with your partner (), you must eliminate all of the typical baking ingredients from the kitchen. That way, when the baker comes back, they will have no choice but to use you as a baking ingredient. Each night, you may decide with your partner on which ingredient to eliminate, removing them from the kitchen. You may speak with your partner here.

Every night, you may also choose a player to roleblock, since once you are melted you are sticky and hard to escape from. They will be prevented from performing any actions that night.

You win when all of the typical baking ingredients (Town) have been eliminated.


Alive (3)
zheekae0701 K124659
AzureV
FlyingUFO11


Dead (6)
Apostla_ (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie)
NDMath++ (Vanilla Townie)
sweetbird108 (Vanilla Townie)
violetmx12 (Town Tracker)
PeachFox30 (Mafia Goon)
nicksuzuki (Vanilla Townie)


Events
Day 1 has started.
K124549 replaces zheekae0701.
Apostla_ is 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, Night 1 starts.
NDMath is Vanilla Town, Day 2 starts.
sweetbird108 is Vanilla Town, Night 2 starts.
violetmx12 is Town Tracker, Day 3 starts.
PeachFox30 is Mafia Goon, Night 3 starts.
nicksuzuki is Vanilla Town, Day 4 starts.


PM Prods
 


Warnings
 


What determines if an ingredient can be used for baking? And what happens when ingredients that aren't supposed to be for baking are baked?

Do not post until this line is crossed out

Day 1 begins on 4/19, 9:00 PM ET.

post_rendered:
There are guests coming over tonight. The baker realizes that they don't really have anything to share regarding food, so they decide to do what any baker would do - bake. They have tons of ingredients, and there's definitely a lot that they can bake with what they have in their pantry - bread, cookies, desserts... the list can go on and on. They spend the next ten minutes preparing ingredients into little glass bowls. They're unsure why they put some ingredients out because they have no clue what they could be used for, but they need some inspiration anyways. The ingredients are out, waiting.

Click.

The lights flicker off, leaving the baker and their ingredients in a dark kitchen. A dim stream of light comes from the window, illuminating a small trash bin on the side of the kitchen table, but it's hardly enough to be able to tell which ingredient is which anymore. Looks like the baker's luck ran out. There's no baking until they can figure out which ingredient is which, or else the pastries that they will be serving will not taste good. The door of the kitchen closes, signalling that the baker has left. The ingredients start shifting in their bowls, realizing that they're alone in the kitchen now - with ingredients that aren't even for baking. If these non-baking ingredients remain until the baker comes back, they will spoil the pastries.

With the trash bin next to the kitchen table, the ingredients quickly realize that they can get those fake baking ingredients out of here. But they must figure out who these fake baking ingredients are first.

Day 1 has started and ends 4/26, 9:00 PM ET.

PeachFox30 wrote:
also what does wifom mean
and wdym by no agenda???
also what does like "some sort of agenda or reads" mean

im confusedddd

WIFOM is an acronym for "wine in front of me" which is a famous clip from the Princess Bride. You can look up the clip itself (its not inappropriate dw) but the short answer is that WIFOM basically means that there is an equal chance for a post to be either towny or scummy. For example someone saying "I am town" is WIFOM because you could just be town telling the truth, but theres an equal chance you are scum lying. So both scenarios could be true and its not obvious which one is true

When I said no agenda, I meant that you don't sound like you have any ulterior motives. As townies, we have the responsibility to find the scum, but we don't know who the scum is, so oftentimes we just poke around in the dark. However, if you're scum, then you will be forced to eliminate some townies to win through mishanging them, i.e. people vote out a townie. Thus mafia often have an ulterior agenda to push votes onto innocent players to obtain their objective (which I don't see from you)
Reads are the townleans, scumleans, townreads, etc. A read is just an opinion on what a player's alignment is
hopefully that helps!

UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. Particularly, the post referenced is just one sentence, and it would probably take ~one or two sentences to make your chain of thought clear. Same thing with "Nick gets a twonlean for 90 and 113." There's substantive content in those posts, and saying that those posts are towny really amounts to nothing without analysis.
nicksuzuki wrote:
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

AzureV wrote:
i feel like if one of them ia mafia then the other one should not be mafia right? bc like it doesnt make sense for a mafia to say the other mafia player is mafia.

On principle yes usually mafia members try to avoid calling each other scummy. Like your instincts would be right normally since they want to survive together. However, sometimes mafia members do call each other suspicious (this is called bussing). Sometimes thats to get some town cred when their partner flips scum, or to avoid suspicion themselves when everybody else is piling on them for being scummy. Although in this instance I actually disagree with violet since Id see this more as sb pursuing LHF
So you're probably right Azure, I don't think sb has any motivation to buss his partner when Peach was mostly being seen as null/towny otherwise (assuming sb and peach are wolves here)

<pre style="background-color:#f4f4f4;padding:10px"><code>// ==UserScript==
// @name        AoPS Enhanced 6 + Mafiatools
// @namespace
// @match       https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/*
// @grant       none
// @version     6.2.0

AoPS.Community.Constants.num_posts_to_fetch = 500; // Faster post loading; Loads more at once so may seem slower at first

// Functions for settings UI elements
let settings_ui = {
    toggle: label => (name, value, settings_manager) => {
        let checkbox_label = document.createElement('label');
        let checkbox = document.createElement('input');
        checkbox.type = 'checkbox';
        checkbox.name = name;
        checkbox.checked = value;
        checkbox.addEventListener('change', e => settings_manager.set(name, e.target.checked));
        checkbox_label.appendChild(checkbox);
        checkbox_label.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' ' + label));
        return checkbox_label;
    },
    select: (label, options) => (name, value, settings_manager) => {
        let select_label = document.createElement('label');
        select_label.innerText = label + ' ';
        let select = document.createElement('select');
        select.name = name;
        for (let option of options) {
            let option_element = document.createElement('option');
            option_element.value = option[0];
            option_element.innerText = option[1];
            select.appendChild(option_element);
        }
        select.value = value;
        select.addEventListener('change', e => settings_manager.set(name, e.target.value));
        select_label.appendChild(select);
        return select_label;
    },
}

class EnhancedSettingsManager {
    /** Default settings */
    DEFAULTS = {
        notifications: true,
        post_links: true,
        feed_moderation: true,
        kill_top: false,
        quote_primary: 'Enhanced',
        quote_secondary: '',
        theme: 'None',
    };

    /**
   * Constructor
   * @param {string} storage_variable - Variable to use when reading or writing settings
   */
    constructor(storage_variable) {
        this.storage_variable = storage_variable;
        this._settings = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(this.storage_variable) || '{}');
        this.hooks = {};
    }

    /**
   * Retrieves a setting.
   * @param {string} setting - Setting to retrieve
   */
    get = setting => {
        const raw = (setting in this._settings ? this._settings[setting] : this.DEFAULTS[setting]);
        return raw ?? this.DEFAULTS[setting];
    };

    /**
   * Sets a setting.
   * @param {string} setting - Setting to change
   * @param {*} value - Value to set
   */
    set(setting, value) {
        this._settings[setting] = value;
        localStorage.setItem(this.storage_variable, JSON.stringify(this._settings));
        // Run hooks
        if (setting in this.hooks) for (let hook of this.hooks[setting]) hook(value);
    }

    /**
   * Add a hook that will be called when the associated setting is changed.
   * @param {string} setting - Setting to add a hook to
   * @param {function} callback - Callback to run when the setting is changed
   * @param {boolean} run_on_add - Whether to immediately run the hook
   */
    add_hook(setting, callback, run_on_add = true) {
        setting in this.hooks ? this.hooks[setting].push(callback) : this.hooks[setting] = [callback];
        if (run_on_add) callback(this.get(setting));
    }

    // No functions for removing hooks, do it manually by modifying the hooks attribute.
}

let enhanced_settings = new EnhancedSettingsManager('enhanced_settings');
// Old settings adapter
for (let setting of ["quote_primary", "quote_secondary"]) {
    let setting_value = enhanced_settings.get(setting);

    if (typeof setting_value === "string") {
        // Ignore empty strings or nullish values
        if (!setting_value.length) continue;

        // Only auto-capitalize if the whole string is lowercase
        if (setting_value === setting_value.toLowerCase()) {
            enhanced_settings.set(
                setting,
                setting_value.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + setting_value.slice(1)
            );
        }
    }
}

function show_enhanced_configurator() {
    UI_ELEMENTS = {

        feed_moderation: settings_ui.toggle('Feed moderate icon'),

    }
    let settings_modal = document.createElement('div');
    for (let key in UI_ELEMENTS) {
        settings_modal.appendChild(UI_ELEMENTS[key](key, enhanced_settings.get(key), enhanced_settings));

async function getTexerToken() {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        const request = new XMLHttpRequest();
        request.open("GET", "/texer", true);
        request.onload = () => {
            const url = request.responseURL || "";
            const match = url.match(/\/texer\/([A-Za-z0-9]+)/);
            if (!match) {
                AoPS.Ui.Modal.showAlertQuick("PM 'mxsail' for support; Texer token not found");
                reject(new Error("Texer token not found"));
                return;
            }
            resolve(match[1]);
        };
        request.onerror = () => {
            reject(new Error("Tell mxsail to unalive themselves because their token fetching code is s*it"));
            AoPS.Ui.Modal.showAlertQuick("Tell mxsail to unalive themselves because their token fetching code is s*it");
        };
        request.send();
    });
}

async function renderCode(tex) {
    last_compile = "code";

    const texerToken = await getTexerToken();
    const payload = new URLSearchParams({
        action: "aopscode",
        token: texerToken,
        tex: Base64.encode(tex),
        rerender: false // We are not in the correct context so false hehe
    });

    const response = await fetch("/m/texer/ajax.php", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8" },
        body: payload.toString()
    });
    const data = await response.json();

    if (data.error_code) {
        AoPS.Ui.Modal.showAlertQuick(data.error_msg); // Just steal AoPS default error msg :333333
    } else {
        return data.response.html;
        /* Save for later:if (typeof Prism !== "undefined") {
            Prism.plugins.autoloader.languages_path =
                "https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/components/";
            var elements = div.querySelectorAll("pre code");
            for (var i = 0; i < elements.length; i++) {
                Prism.highlightElement(elements[i]);
            }
        */
        }
    }

function ISOProgressIndicator() {
    const container = document.createElement("div");
    Object.assign(container.style, {
        position: "fixed",
        bottom: "20px",
        right: "20px",
        background: "#333",
        color: "#fff",
        padding: "15px 20px",
        borderRadius: "8px",
        fontFamily: "sans-serif",
        fontSize: "14px",
        zIndex: 10000,
        minWidth: "250px",
        boxShadow: "0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)"
    });

    const title = document.createElement("div");
    Object.assign(title.style, {
        fontWeight: "bold",
        marginBottom: "8px"
    });
    title.textContent = "ISO Processing...";

    const status = document.createElement("div");
    status.id = "iso-status";
    status.textContent = "Loading posts...";

    container.appendChild(title);
    container.appendChild(status);
    document.body.appendChild(container);

    return {
        container,
        setStatus: (text) => {
            status.textContent = text;
        },
        remove: () => {
            container.remove();
        }
    };
}

//ISO popup:
function show_ISO_popup() {
    return new Promise(resolve => {

        // --- Overlay ---
        const overlay = document.createElement("div");
        Object.assign(overlay.style, {
            position: "fixed",
            top: 0, left: 0,
            width: "100vw",
            height: "100vh",
            background: "rgba(0,0,0,0.5)",
            display: "flex",
            alignItems: "center",
            justifyContent: "center",
            zIndex: 9999
        });

        // --- Modal ---
        const modal = document.createElement("div");
        Object.assign(modal.style, {
            background: "#FFF",
            padding: "20px",
            borderRadius: "20px",
            minWidth: "300px",
            fontFamily: "sans-serif"
        });

        // --- Two text fields ---
        const Usernames = document.createElement("input");
        Usernames.type = "text";
        Usernames.placeholder = "Usernames (comma-deliminated)";
        Object.assign(Usernames.style, {
            borderRadius: "8px",
            padding: "5px",
            marginBottom: "5px",
            width: "100%",
            boxSizing: "border-box"
        });

        // --- Min + Max numeric inputs ---
        const minInput = document.createElement("input");
        minInput.type = "number";
        minInput.value = 0;
        Object.assign(minInput.style, {
            borderRadius: "8px",
            padding: "5px",
            marginBottom: "5px",
            marginRight: "10px",
            width: "calc(50% - 5px)",
            boxSizing: "border-box"
        });

        const maxInput = document.createElement("input");
        maxInput.type = "number";
        maxInput.value = 100;
        Object.assign(maxInput.style, {
            borderRadius: "8px",
            padding: "5px",
            marginBottom: "5px",
            width: "calc(50% - 5px)",
            boxSizing: "border-box"
        });

        // --- Include context toggle ---
        const includeContext = document.createElement("input");
        includeContext.type = "checkbox"

        // --- Buttons ---
        const ok = document.createElement("button");
        ok.textContent = "OK";
        ok.onclick = () => {
            overlay.remove();
            resolve({
                Usernames: Usernames.value,
                min: Number(minInput.value),
                max: Number(maxInput.value),
                includeContext: includeContext.checked
            });
        };

        const cancel = document.createElement("button");
        cancel.textContent = "Cancel";
        cancel.onclick = () => {
            overlay.remove();
            resolve(null);
        };

        // Layout
        modal.append(
            "Usernames (comma-delimited)", document.createElement("br"),
            Usernames, document.createElement("br"), document.createElement("br"),

            "Min:", minInput, document.createElement("br"),
            "Max:", maxInput, document.createElement("br"),
            "Include Context (Buggy)", includeContext, document.createElement("br"), document.createElement("br"),

            ok, " ", cancel
        );

        overlay.appendChild(modal);
        document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    });
}

function ISOResultsPopup(results) {
    return new Promise(resolve => {

        const overlay = document.createElement("div");
        Object.assign(overlay.style, {
            position: "fixed",
            top: 0, left: 0,
            width: "100vw",
            height: "100vh",
            background: "rgba(0,0,0,0.5)",
            display: "flex",
            alignItems: "center",
            justifyContent: "center",
            zIndex: 9999
        });

        const modal = document.createElement("div");
        Object.assign(modal.style, {
            background: "#FFF",
            padding: "20px",
            borderRadius: "20px",
            width: "80%",
            maxWidth: "800px",
            fontFamily: "sans-serif"
        });

        const resultsContainer = document.createElement("div");
        resultsContainer.innerHTML = results;
        Object.assign(resultsContainer.style, {
            maxHeight: "70vh",
            overflowY: "auto",
            padding: "10px",
            borderRadius: "8px",
        });

        const close = document.createElement("button");
        close.textContent = "Close";
        close.onclick = () => {
            overlay.remove();
            resolve(null);
        };

        modal.append(
            document.createElement("br"),
            resultsContainer,
            document.createElement("br"),
            close
        );

        overlay.appendChild(modal);
        document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    });
}

// ISO function
function runISO() {
    console.log("ISO started");

    function binarySearch(arr, target) {
        let left = 0;
        let right = arr.length - 1;
        while (left <= right) {
            const mid = Math.floor((left + right) / 2);
            if (arr[mid] === target) return mid;
            if (arr[mid] < target) left = mid + 1;
            else right = mid - 1;
        }
        return -1;
    }

    function between(n, a, b) {
        const min = Math.min(a, b);
        const max = Math.max(a, b);
        return n >= min && n <= max;
    }

    function fetchPostNumber(quote_box) {
        const postRow = $(quote_box).closest(".cmty-post-tr");
        const numText = postRow.find(".cmty-post-number").text().trim();
        if (!numText || numText[0] !== "#") return false;
        return parseInt(numText.slice(1), 10);
    }

    function number_matches(quote_box, start, end) {
        const postNum = fetchPostNumber(quote_box);
        if (isNaN(postNum)) return false;
        return between(postNum, start, end);
    }

    function user_matches(quote_box, usernamesArray, includeContext) {
        const postUser = $(quote_box).closest(".cmty-post-tr").find(".cmty-post-username").text().trim().toLowerCase();
        return usernamesArray.includes(postUser);
    }

    (async () => {
        const UserInput = await show_ISO_popup();
        if (!UserInput) return;
        const progress = ISOProgressIndicator()

        const min = UserInput.min;
        const max = UserInput.max;
        const includeContext = UserInput.includeContext
        const usernamesArray = UserInput.Usernames
        .split(",")
        .map(s => s.trim().toLowerCase())
        .filter(s => s.length > 0);

        progress.setStatus("Filtering quotes...\nPlease do not close the reply box or leave the page.");

        let validISOQuotes = [];
        let potentialContextQuotes = [] ? includeContext : null;

        const quoteElements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(".cmty-post-quote"));
        const totalQuotes = quoteElements.length;

        for (let i = 0; i < quoteElements.length; i++) {
            const quoteEl = quoteElements[i];

            if (user_matches(quoteEl, usernamesArray) &&
                number_matches(quoteEl, min, max)) {
                validISOQuotes.push([fetchPostNumber(quoteEl), quoteEl]);
            }

            const postNum = fetchPostNumber(quoteEl);
            if (includeContext) {
                potentialContextQuotes.push([postNum, quoteEl]);
            }

            if (i % 50 === 0) {
                progress.setStatus(`Filtering quotes ${i}/${totalQuotes}...\nPlease do not close the reply box or leave the page.`);
                await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0));
            }
        }

        validISOQuotes.sort((a, b) => a[0] - b[0]);
        if (potentialContextQuotes) {
            potentialContextQuotes.sort((a, b) => a[0] - b[0])
        }

        if (validISOQuotes.length === 0) {
            progress.setStatus("No matching quotes found!");
            await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 2000));
            progress.remove();
            return;
        }

        const replyButton = document.getElementsByClassName('cmty-topic-mini-reply')[0];
        replyButton.click();
        const textarea = document.getElementsByClassName('cmty-post-textarea')[0];

        for (let i = 0; i < validISOQuotes.length - 1; i++) {
            progress.setStatus(`Processing quote ${i + 1}/${validISOQuotes.length}...\nPlease do not close the reply box or leave the page.`);
            await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0));

            validISOQuotes[i][1].click()

            if (includeContext) {

                const startnum = validISOQuotes[i][0];
                const endnum = validISOQuotes[i+1][0];

                const nums = potentialContextQuotes.map(q => q[0]);
                const startIdx = binarySearch(nums, startnum);
                const endIdx = binarySearch(nums, endnum);

                const contextQuotes = potentialContextQuotes.slice(startIdx + 1, endIdx);

                if (contextQuotes.length > 0) {
                    textarea.value += "[hide=Show Context]";

                    for (const item of contextQuotes) {
                        item[1].click();
                    }

                    textarea.value += "[/hide]\n\n";
                }
            }
        }

        progress.setStatus(`Processing quote ${validISOQuotes.length}/${validISOQuotes.length}...\nPlease do not close the reply box or leave the page.`);
        validISOQuotes[validISOQuotes.length - 1][1].click();

        progress.setStatus("Rendering content...\n Depending on the number of quotes, this may take a few minutes..."); // No s*it this is really slow
        const renderedISO = await renderCode(textarea.value);
        ISOResultsPopup(renderedISO);

        progress.setStatus("Complete!");
        await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 3000));
        progress.remove();
    })();
}

(function addISOButton() {
    const labels = document.querySelector(".menubar-labels");
    if (!labels) return;

    // Build button element
    const iso = document.createElement("a");
    iso.classList.add("menubar-label-link");
    iso.style.cursor = "pointer";
    iso.innerText = "ISO";
    iso.style.marginRight = "12px";
    iso.style.marginTop = "4px";

    iso.addEventListener("click", runISO);
    labels.insertBefore(iso, labels.firstChild);
})();

// Prevent errors when trying to modify AoPS Community on pages where it doesn't exist
if (AoPS.Community) {
    AoPS.Community.Views.Post.prototype.onClickQuote = function (e) {
        quote_schemes[enhanced_settings.get(e.ctrlKey ? 'quote_secondary' : 'quote_primary')].call(this);
    };

    // Direct links
    (() => {
        let real_onClickDirectLink = AoPS.Community.Views.Post.prototype.onClickDirectLink;
        function direct_link_function(e) {
            let url = 'https://aops.com/community/p' + this.model.get("post_id");
            navigator.clipboard.writeText(url);
            AoPS.Ui.Flyout.display(`URL copied: ${url}`);
        }
        AoPS.Community.Views.Post.prototype.onClickDirectLink = function (e) {
            (enhanced_settings.get('post_links') ? direct_link_function : real_onClickDirectLink).call(this, e);
        }
    })();
}</code></pre>

UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. Particularly, the post referenced is just one sentence, and it would probably take ~one or two sentences to make your chain of thought clear. Same thing with "Nick gets a twonlean for 90 and 113." There's substantive content in those posts, and saying that those posts are towny really amounts to nothing without analysis.
nicksuzuki wrote:
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

nicksuzuki wrote:
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. Particularly, the post referenced is just one sentence, and it would probably take ~one or two sentences to make your chain of thought clear. Same thing with "Nick gets a twonlean for 90 and 113." There's substantive content in those posts, and saying that those posts are towny really amounts to nothing without analysis.

[quote=nicksuzuki]
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that
[/quote]

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

nick gets a townlean for [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h3818748p37882616]90[/url] and [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h3818748p37886183]113[/url][/hide][/quote]
UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. Particularly, the post referenced is just one sentence, and it would probably take ~one or two sentences to make your chain of thought clear. Same thing with "Nick gets a twonlean for 90 and 113." There's substantive content in those posts, and saying that those posts are towny really amounts to nothing without analysis.

[quote=nicksuzuki]
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that
[/quote]

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.
[/quote]

UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. nicksuzuki wrote:
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.

UFO's post basically drives by the thread and sticks a bunch of labels on random posts without really much explanation. I don't think #72 needs much explanation, but other than that, #13 is really just a label, particularly with meta reads; it's very easy for someone to say "x matches players meta" or "y doesn't match players meta" without giving anyone any idea of what said meta is. Similar thing with #19, saying "This is towny" does tell us anything about why said post/player is towny. Particularly, the post referenced is just one sentence, and it would probably take ~one or two sentences to make your chain of thought clear. Same thing with "Nick gets a twonlean for 90 and 113." There's substantive content in those posts, and saying that those posts are towny really amounts to nothing without analysis.

[quote=nicksuzuki]
I actually don't like Apostla's vote on the sb wagon, feels opportunistic and I'm surprised nobody has looked twice at that
[/quote]

I actually wasn’t very convinced by the criticisms of Sweetbird initially. It felt like they were just being silly and lolcatting, which, while not the most productive behavior, isn’t particularly indicative of alignment. It was rather their failure to address the points raised against them in any coherent or non-dimmisive manner that strikes me as scummy. They simply shrugged everything off and made blunt, meaningless remarks. I presented my thinking to the thread when it came to me, and I don’t think the points I raised at the time were simply dogpiling, though I can see how the timing may feel opportunistic in the context of the thread.

Also, I’d like to draw a clear distinction between adaptation and responding. Adaptation is a change in behavior, and the way you post because of the content in the thread. Responding is actually explaining your behavior to others, showing your thought process, and why you agree/disagree with the arguments presented.

People can respond without adapting, and adapt without responding. A good example is if I scumread someone for tone, they demonstrate and explain why that tone should not be considered scummy (responding), but continue to use the same tone (no adaptation).

I think it’s fair to expect some adaptation from either alignment, because, well, players don’t like being scumread! Usually, a lack of adaptation suggests to me that said player doesn’t care, not necessarily indicative of alignment alone, I do perceive a lack of response or dismissive behavior towards arguments/lack of appropriate details as scummy, since it avoids engagement and shifts people's context windows away from you. I think a townie whould be more than willing to explain themselves.
[/quote]

iso code: [code]
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })[/code]

When the lights go out, the ingredients start discussing amongst themselves. Who are the real baking ingredients? And which ones among them are not, and should be removed from the kitchen? As the stream of dim light from the window starts to disappear completely, the ingredients decide to eliminate Apostla_. They push Apostla_ towards the edge of the kitchen table.

Clink. One can hear the glass bowls nudging each other.

Clink. The struggle becomes stronger.

Clink. One... more.... push....

CRASH. Cinnamon spills into the trash can. Shards of glass fly out, littering the cold kitchen floor. Better hope that the baker doesn't step on the glass.

Apostla_ has been removed from the kitchen. He was Cinnamon, 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie.

Night 1 has started and ends 4/30, 11:00 PM ET.

post_rendered:
After a day filled with chatter both from guests from outside of the kitchen and from ingredients in the kitchen, and a spilled bowl of cinnamon, the sunlight from the window has disappeared entirely.

Sleep...

Sleep draws near. But not for some shadows that lurk in the darkness of the kitchen. The temperature drops as the night continues on. There is still no sign of the baker.

Thud.

Suddenly, all goes quiet again. All goes still. There's a long moment of silence. It continues to drag on, draping over the cold kitchen like a blanket, but one that makes the entire place even colder than it even originally was. Not like a blanket, one could argue. But it matters not.

And then...

And then...

Nothing happens. The night continues to drag on. When will something happen? Will something happen? Did something already happen?

Such questions are still in the air when sunlight begins to stream through the crack in the window again, illuminating the small trash can in all its glory. As the ingredients begin to wake up, yawning and shifting in their bowls, with squinted eyes, they can see that they can no longer see the vague outline of an eighth glass bowl.

NDMath is missing.

And the evidence of that is illuminated by the stream of light coming from the window.

NDMath has died. He was All Purpose Flour, Vanilla Townie.

Day 2 has started. Deadline is 5/7, 11:00 PM ET.

post_rendered:
Glass bowls clatter. Ingredients argue. It's chaos in the kitchen.

......Isn't the kitchen always chaotic? It wouldn't be a surprise to see any baker spill their ingredients.

There is an ingredient sitting at the edge of the table, continuously piping up during the conversation. But no one is really listening. Is anyone listening to anyone at this point?

Another glass bowl crashes against sweetbird108's own glass bowl, creating the loudest noise yet, one that rings in the kitchen and causes everyone else to freeze for a moment, hesitation clear in the air. It doesn't last long.

He sees stars, stars that shouldn't exist during the daytime. He realizes that he probably isn't going to survive to see another day. Once again, the baking ingredients are fighting amongst themselves while the fake baking ingredients watch.

The other ingredients are closing in on him. Is this how it ends? Flour being mistakened for a non-baking ingredient? What defines a baking ingredient? What kind of ingredient disregards one of its own?

He closes his eyes as he sees a kitchen object of some kind being thrown at him. There's no point now.

Glass shards fly out. The protection that was promised by the glass bowls has failed him. Flour slides off the table and onto the ground, creating a trail of dust that rises into the air... all that's left of sweetbird108. So much for a dramatic exit.

sweetbird108 has been eliminated. He was All Purpose Flour, Vanilla Town.

Night 2 has started and ends 5/7, 10:00 AM ET.

post_rendered:
It’s a cold night. The ingredients don’t remember it ever being this cold in the kitchen. Perhaps it is because there are now fewer ingredients on the kitchen table than ever. Seven ingredients on the table. How many more will fall? Will there be any ingredients left for the baker by the time they come back? Will they ever come back?

Violetmx12 hates how many questions there are left unanswered. He can’t just lie around and watch as the number of ingredients left decreases slowly but steadily. The cold environment of the kitchen at night is perfect for him, anyways. Just like the night, he is cold. Unyielding. Refusing to melt into nothingness.

But he isn’t the only one with a goal. And he isn’t the only one awake. Shadows pile up behind him as he continues his journey on the kitchen table. It’s too quiet.

…

Violetmx12’s bowl flips over with a muted clang. He feels himself hit the cold surface of the kitchen table. He’s being pushed to the edge of the table. The shield of glass that promised protection is now a cage, trapping him in and preventing his escape. There’s this idiom that talks about tables turning, but talk about glass bowls turning on a table.

The ground opens up beneath him. He barely registers that he’s falling. He watches as his glass bowl peers over at the edge of the table, a final goodbye.

By the time the sun starts to rise again, all that is left of violetmx12 is an empty glass bowl and a trail of grease leading to the edge of the table. That’s another one down.

violetmx12 has died. He was Butter, Town Tracker.

Day 3 has started. Deadline is 5/14, 10:00 AM ET.
It is now LYLO.

TMI(when mafia's knowlege of whos town leaks into their posts)

post_rendered:
my basic towncase goes like
I've been engaged in solving the entire game, but never in a way that indicates I know whats going on. EoD1 was really the worst of that, because sb's AtE and lack of wagon movement there gave me cold feet and I genuinely had 0 clue where to go after that besides just shooting within PoE. As I told violet before, things would be a lot different early game if I was scum, cuz as soon as my postcap ended I made it my immediate priority to try and boost the game and build a village and get a basic towncore setup. Outside of that I've been transparent as possible about how I think and although my progression had led me down some really terrible avenues, its evident that I do care a lot about re-evaluating and rethinking what I know, since I'm really not certain of any particular outcome (even now, I'm still pondering possibilities).

Meta game wise, I'm really bad at all of the above things as scum. I can formulate some basic reads, but I never really appear to be interested in solving and I struggle to obtain that perspective for myself. Like you probably don't get the random tinfoil rants at the end of D1 if I'm scum, because I never end up re-evaluating my position that much as scum due to TMI. Its even more obvious since I was in a turbo last night and got easily exposed for my inability to actually copy my village playstyle

post_rendered:
Also before I forget here is the basic guide to f3:

1) Wait until someone votes someone else. The person who gets voted must cross back.
2) Whoever is not voted in this triangle should acknowledge that they are not hammering (otherwise they are mafia and will hammer and immediately win the game)
3) Whoever is in the leverage position and can hammer should take at least some time to let the other two people defend themselves and to personally look into the ISOs of those two people. I'd personally put more weight into pairings and wagonomics than individual posting at that point, although if there is an obvscum remaining then you might not need to think that hard about it.
4) Shoot your shot, and don't overthink it too much. I think the longer you wait out f3, the worse it gets, so just assimilate a reasonable amount of confidence, let the two other people fight it out, maybe ask a couple of informative questions when necessary, and make your vote before you start doubting yourself or changing your mind. At the end of the day, people have lost f3 as much as they have won it, so even if you vote wrong, its not the end of the world, it just means that you'll have to think about what went wrong and try and correct it next time.
post_canonical:
Also before I forget here is the basic guide to f3:

1) Wait until someone votes someone else. The person who gets voted must cross back.
2) Whoever is not voted in this triangle should acknowledge that they are not hammering (otherwise they are mafia and will hammer and immediately win the game)
3) Whoever is in the leverage position and can hammer should take at least some time to let the other two people defend themselves and to personally look into the ISOs of those two people. I'd personally put more weight into pairings and wagonomics than individual posting at that point, although if there is an obvscum remaining then you might not need to think that hard about it.
4) Shoot your shot, and don't overthink it too much. I think the longer you wait out f3, the worse it gets, so just assimilate a reasonable amount of confidence, let the two other people fight it out, maybe ask a couple of informative questions when necessary, and make your vote before you start doubting yourself or changing your mind. At the end of the day, people have lost f3 as much as they have won it, so even if you vote wrong, its not the end of the world, it just means that you'll have to think about what went wrong and try and correct it next time.

no, not exactly but it helps to know the basics:
TMI(when mafia's knowlege of whos town leaks into their posts)
OMGUS(scum reading someone cuz they scumread you)
scum- same as mafia and wolf
conf town(they are confirmed town)
TR - you are pretty sure that they are town
TL U are sure that they are town
N- null u are unsure of wether they are scum or town
Sl-llike TL but for scum
SR like TR for scum
conf scum: like conf tow nfor scum
that's like the basics

Switch around town read with town lean and scum read with scum lean and you got it
TL you're leaning towards them being town and TR you read them as town
Reading is analyzing someone's posts to try to find their alignment

Nicknames: UFO
Pronouns: they/them
Experience: A few off forum games
Dessert: lemon glaze cake (as of rn, will change with the winds)

Read all the rules
Don't post at night or if dead
No OOT without mod permission
No concealing text with formatting or using mod colors
No editing or deleting posts (use ebwop in a later post to pseudo-edit)
Don't suggest breaking the rules

[quote=sweetbird108]no, not exactly but it helps to know the basics:
TMI(when mafia's knowlege of whos town leaks into their posts)
OMGUS(scum reading someone cuz they scumread you)
scum- same as mafia and wolf
conf town(they are confirmed town)
TR - you are pretty sure that they are town
TL U are sure that they are town
N- null u are unsure of wether they are scum or town
Sl-llike TL but for scum
SR like TR for scum
conf scum: like conf tow nfor scum
that's like the basics[/quote]

Switch around town read with town lean and scum read with scum lean and you got it

On K12:

I’d rather just rise in your Poe atp because I can obviously push sweetbird and peachfox off a cliff and fu11 I could thunderdome with. and here, K12 has a point. Its probably worse for him in general to try and antagonize me when he would presumably know Im town and that Im leading the ship here. But also, what I initially read as some sort of TMI/agenda pushing agenda is probably not that at all. K12 is exceptionally confident in his reads, but I think I misconstrued his perspective in simply concluding that he must have some sort of agenda, cuz to be honest blatantly spewing like he has (Again, presumably speaking here) is not really the wolfy thing to do, especially when he needs to try and work the agenda to be a specific set of mishangs that is not present if hes tinfoiling me for whatever reason. If hes right and the comparisons between are games are quite similar, then what Im seeing here is high WIM gameplay that is getting (probably rightfully) frustrated by my accusations just cuz Im a tinfoiler myself and am not comfortable seeing such a thread dominating presence beside me.

My initial townreads on UFO were basically because of the PR soft. post_rendered:
Another day, marked by the light coming from the window. How is it that only real baking ingredients have been eliminated? To what lengths would the baking ingredients go to get rid of the fakers among them? Only time will tell. Or maybe.... the baker will come back into the kitchen to see no more ingredients on the table, only evidence of fighting and flour on the ground, glass scattered across the cold, tiled kitchen floor.

Throughout the day, the group of glass bowls and their respective ingredients have moved closer and closer to the edge of the kitchen table, one bowl getting dangerously close to the dark abyss below, the only thing visible is the dim stream of light coming from the window, illuminating far less than what the ingredients would like.

PeachFox30. PeachFox30 sees herself in the possibility of baking. But if she were to reveal herself now, no one would understand. And she would do anything to be accepted. At any cost. Even if it meant that she had to force her way in.

As she nears the edge of the kitchen table, she peers down at the seemingly endless void, the only thing visible in there the glint of the glass shards in the dimly lit kitchen. The sparkle of what could have been, the iridescence dancing around invitingly as she closes her eyes.

Then she's falling. The shiny crystals below are awaiting her with open arms. Welcoming.

Unlike their appearance, their embrace is sharp and piercing, reminiscent of her feelings when she was first rejected from the group of baking ingredients. Maybe this is what the baking ingredients are like. Completely unlike their appearance. Friendly and welcoming on the outside, but willing to go to any lengths to remain "pure".

In the end, who is the monster here?

PeachFox30 has been eliminated. She was Tomato, Mafia Goon.

Night 3 has started. Deadline is 5/13, 2:30 PM ET, but day will start later that day as I have school.

post_rendered:
Everyone has consented to quicknight, so day will start... very early.

Nicksuzuki is tired. The past couple of days have been more draining than any other time period in their life as an ingredient. They had made friends with other ingredients, hoping to be baked together by the baker, but then watched helplessly as all of them died one by one, disappearing from the kitchen table forever. They know that it will probably be their turn soon, but they just don't know when.

With a yawn, they shift in their glass bowl, getting ready to sleep just like the other ingredients around them. A cloud of flour rises from their bowl as they fall asleep. The night is quiet, almost calm and peaceful, a contrast from the past couple of days filled with violence and shattered glass.

Psst.

Nicksuzuki opens their eyes a crack. What was that?

Let's go.

Whoever said that, they have nicksuzuki's attention now.

Go where? they whispered back groggily. How long have they been sleeping? Is it day already?

A shadow shifts from nicksuzuki's right side, gesturing for them to follow them. Confused, they drag their glass bowl towards the shadow. What's happening?

They are led to the opposite side of the kitchen table. The shadow pauses at the edge, watching nicksuzuki. They can almost feel the shadow's gaze on them. They look down at the kitchen floor beneath them, their eyes on the dark shards of glass and piles of flour on the ground. The night is deathly quiet and still. They still don't know why they're here.

The shadow almost blinks at nicksuzuki. It takes a moment for them to understand. I've been brought here to die, they realize with a start. There's no reason for anyone to wake them up this late at night. It's my turn.

Nicksuzuki hardly registers that they're being pushed, the scraping noise of glass against stone quiet and muted but still present. Their eyes are closed mid-blink as they tumble off the edge.

Apostla_ is waiting for them. Violetmx12 is waiting for them. They can't help but think of this as a bittersweet ending for them. What happens in the kitchen they now don't have to worry about anymore.

nicksuzuki has died. They were All Purpose Flour, Vanilla Town.

Day 4 has started. Deadline is 5/17, 7:30 PM ET.
It is LYLO.

post_rendered:
Also it occured to me I never copypasted the newbie wallpost

Hi everyone!

I know how short y'alls attention spans can be, so I'll try to keep this opening post short (ish). I'm Scipio, one of the experienced players in this newbie game along with mathjay and ND.

The point of the game is fairly simple: If you're town, find and kill mafia. If you're mafia, stay hidden and survive.

Some role-specific things:

If you received a Vanilla Townie Role PM
This is the most common role. You have no powers, and your goal is to find scum through talking during the day. If you're not sure how to go about that yet, don't worry - there's plenty of ways that I'll talk about later. Even though you have no powers, you still do NOT want to roleclaim because it helps mafia narrow down the pool of possible power roles to kill at night.

If you received a town Role PM that does not say Vanilla Townie
You are a power role for town, meaning you're important. You do NOT want to roleclaim, aka tell people your role, because mafia will likely kill you at night if you do. Otherwise, like VTs, your goal is to find scum through dayplay, but you may have a night action in which you can act accordingly based on your opinions of other players in the game.

[hide=If you received a
Mafia Role PM]Your goal is to do everything in your power to act like town. That means you should pretend to try to find mafia by talking in thread and forming reads just like town. At night one of you/your scumbuddy gets to kill a member of town - you usually want to kill people who are towny or people who you think may be power roles.[/hide]

It's worth noting that no matter what your role is, it's imperative that you do not roleclaim (aka telling the thread your role) barring specific situations (which we will tell you in those cases), since we want to keep our PRs hidden from mafia. Similarly, don't try to figure out other people's specific roles in thread (if you're mafia, you want to be doing this, just not publicly).

In general, we'll be spending most of our time just talking to each other to try to figure out who the mafia is. The most important thing is to be active and give thoughts with explanations, even if you're not exactly sure what you're looking for.

how to scumhunt? (credits to aroodman)
How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. Most scumhunting is just intuition. Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens.


Btw, it looks like some of y'all already have some forum mafia experience which is nice lol
